name: marketplace-system
summary: A task-first marketplace archetype that prioritizes search, taxonomy, cards, and trust surfaces over long-form narrative storytelling.
goals: Move users into search or browse flows quickly.
Keep dense inventory or taxonomy legible across desktop and mobile.
Use a small set of repeatable shells to support scale and personalization.
layout_rules: Make search or browsing intent the dominant first-screen action.
Use desktop space for breadth and discovery, but shift mobile toward quick entry and high-signal cards.
Organize dense discovery content with tabs, columns, and repeatable card shells instead of decorative wrappers.
typography_rules: Use neutral product typography that supports speed and scan clarity.
Keep section headlines functional and concise.
Use quieter secondary text for metadata and pricing details without sacrificing readability.
component_rules: Invest in the search shell, inventory card, and category tab system before secondary storytelling modules.
Use generous radius, stable image ratios, and clear card metadata ordering.
Treat banners, trust modals, and locale controls as utility surfaces, not decorative interruptions.
motion_rules: Keep motion restrained and state-driven around sheets, tabs, search expansion, and card actions.
Do not let overlays or promos compete with the booking or browse flow.
Prioritize responsiveness and clarity over expressive transitions.
copy_rules: Keep labels short, direct, and operational.
Use policy or trust copy only where it removes friction from the next user action.
Let taxonomy and inventory do most of the persuasion work.
accessibility_rules: Preserve skip links, semantic navigation, and tab semantics in highly customized UI shells.
Ensure card controls and overlays have explicit labels and dismiss paths.
Do not rely on color, badges, or hover alone to communicate state.
performance_rules: Defer noncritical media and offscreen video aggressively.
Keep card grids responsive before layering on personalization or promo surfaces.
Treat mobile overlay stacking as a performance and usability budget, not just a marketing slot.
anti_patterns: Using too many promotional overlays on mobile.
Overloading the first screen with taxonomy before a clear search or browse action exists.
Letting dense destination or inventory modules collapse into clutter on smaller screens.
Copying a signature marketplace accent system too literally from another brand.
notes: This first version is primarily derived from Airbnb, so future marketplace research batches should broaden and refine it.
It is best suited to search-first products with repeatable inventory cards and category navigation.
